mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) signals strong labor market conditions that boost corporate earnings and consumer spending, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods of PAYEMS growth are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in CPIAUCSL above 0.5% signals rising inflation pressures that force central banks to tighten policy, reducing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite CPIAUCSL increases or Rank IC > 0.05 over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in industrial production (INDPRO) and declining federal funds rates (FEDFUNDS) indicate accommodative monetary policy supporting strong manufacturing activity, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative when both INDPRO and FEDFUNDS meet the trigger conditions or interaction effect size < 0.15 in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: A housing starts (HOUST) level above 2,000 thousand units signals strong residential demand that stimulates construction-related sectors, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX returns are negative in 60%+ of periods when HOUST exceeds 2000 or Rank IC < 0.02 over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production (INDPRO) indicates weakening manufacturing activity that reduces corporate profits, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in > 50% of INDPRO decline periods or interaction effect size < 0.10 in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) above 3.5% signals monetary tightening that increases borrowing costs, reducing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX returns are positive in > 50% of periods when FEDFUNDS exceeds 3.5% or Rank IC > 0.04 over 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A declining unemployment rate (UNRATE) below 5% indicates a tight labor market that drives wage inflation and corporate margin pressures, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX returns are positive in > 50% of periods when UNRATE < 5% or interaction effect size < 0.12 in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in residential construction (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) indicates strong demand for housing and related sectors, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative when both RSAFS and HOUST meet the trigger conditions or interaction effect size < 0.18 in out-of-sample tests.
